Woodlands Manor Farm • The Animals

Meet the animals

From Pringle the cheeky Dartmoor pony to Growler the farm cat — every animal at Woodlands has a name, a personality, and a story.

About About Woodlands On the Farm The Animals Things to Do Places to Eat Little Extras Inventory Dog Rules

Ruth & the animals

Meet the family

We love all our animals on the farm — they are part of the Woodlands Manor Farm family. Come and meet them all on the Feed the Animals tour with Ruth, which runs every Wednesday and Sunday morning. No booking needed — just turn up.

Pringle
Pure Dartmoor Pony

Pringle wins the cheekiest animal on the farm award, hands down. A special pure Dartmoor pony who spent his first 6 months with the herd on the moors. Known for picking up anything not locked down, knocking fences down and winding up every other animal on the farm with his playful antics.

Lulu
Mini Shetland Pony

The kindest, sweetest mini pony you will ever meet. She never tires of meeting little people, loves to be groomed and made a fuss of. Lulu loves the summer season — she particularly enjoys the pony experience sessions, where she gets to be made extremely beautiful and go on walks with the children.

Merlin
Welsh Cob

The elder statesman of the herd and the boss. A lovely boy but can be a little grumpy if not fed first or given the respect he deserves. His favourite pastime is galloping through fields — that is when he is at his happiest.

Zap & Sparky
Pygmy Goats

Often seen playing chase in the field. They love the feed the animals routine with guests, where they get lots of fuss and are fed by hand. Pure entertainment — watch them tackle the adventure course with treats for motivation.

Napoleon, Henry & Erebus
Alpacas

The three amigos — the most inquisitive animals on the farm. Napoleon is a Suri Alpaca with a beautiful dreadlock fleece. Erebus and Henry are Huacaya Alpacas who look like massive teddy bears. They love carrots (finely cut — only bottom teeth!) and will come over to say hello to everyone.

Lucas & Layla
Kune Kune Pigs

Brother and sister — firm favourites on the feed the animals rounds. Playful, kind and love a tummy tickle or a mud bath in summer. Lucas is very good at escaping his pen to find longer grass, while Layla stays behind and enjoys the peace and quiet.

Cutie Pie, Little Z, Twinkle & Rosario
Sheep

Rosario is a survivor — hand-reared after a difficult birth and now more like a dog than a sheep. Cutie Pie was the cutest of the 2022 lambs. Little Z has always been the odd one out — we love her for it.

Ralf & Molly
Rabbits

Ralf is a Mini Lop and Molly is a Lion Lop. Best of friends who sleep curled around one another. We are convinced Ralf is the brother of Peter Rabbit — he loves carrots and is constantly plotting an escape into the allotment.

Growler
Farm Cat — Chief Ratter

Growler adopted us in 2022, making his way over from a nearby farm. Spoilt rotten ever since. Loves his status as chief ratter but most of all loves fuss and sitting on people’s laps. Very happy indeed.

Sundays & Wednesdays with Ruth

Feed the Animals tour

Every Sunday and Wednesday morning, Ruth takes cottage and yurt guests on a tour of all the animals. You will feed, groom and get up close with the ponies, alpacas, goats, pigs, sheep, rabbits and chickens. No booking needed — just meet in the courtyard on the morning. This is consistently the most-mentioned highlight in guest reviews.

Book an experience

A 17th-century farm in the Coombe Valley — cottages, yurts, heated pool and the warmest Cornish welcome.
